_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The North Carolina Register functions as the State's mandatory public notice vehicle for all administrative rulemaking under the North Carolina Administrative Procedure Act (G.S. Chapter 150B). It is not itself a regulation directed at private-sector freight operators; rather, it is the official gazette through which North Carolina state agencies and occupational licensing boards publish proposed rules that may affect those operators.

For a freight-forwarder workspace operating in North Carolina, the compliance-chain position is that of an **interested person and regulated-party monitor**, not a filing party. State agencies and occupational licensing boards are the regulated filers under G.S. 150B-21.2; private businesses such as freight forwarders are members of the public who have the right to comment on proposed rules, demand public hearings under certain conditions, and submit written objections to the Rules Review Commission if a rule threatens to impose undue burdens.

The workspace's exposure arises indirectly: any NC agency rulemaking that touches road transport permitting, cargo vehicle weight/dimension rules, occupational licensing for customs or logistics personnel, hazardous-materials handling, or warehouse/bonded-facility licensing will first appear as a proposed rule notice in the Register. Under G.S. 150B-21.2, the comment period must be open for at least 60 days after publication, giving the workspace an actionable window to respond before rules become final.

The Rules Division of OAH performs legal analysis and administrative work in the review, compilation, production, and publication of both the NC Register and the NC Administrative Code, and provides administrative support to the Rules Review Commission (RRC). The RRC is the executive agency created by the General Assembly charged with reviewing and approving rules adopted by state agencies. Confirmed final rules, after RRC approval, take effect on the first day of the following month and are codified in the North Carolina Administrative Code (NCAC). The workspace should treat the NCAC as the binding operative source and the Register as the advance-warning system.

**Role placement requiring legal confirmation:** Whether the workspace, in any NC-based bonded warehouse or drayage operation, constitutes a "person" with standing to petition an agency to adopt, amend, or repeal a rule under G.S. 150B-20 should be confirmed with NC-licensed counsel.

*Source: NC OAH Rules Division, oah.nc.gov/rules-division; G.S. Chapter 150B (NC General Assembly, ncleg.gov/Laws/GeneralStatuteSections/Chapter150B); Participating in the Rulemaking Process, NC OAH, https://www.oah.nc.gov/rules-division/participating-rulemaking-process*
